channel.title: Morning Agriculture
channel.description: Delivered every Monday by 10 a.m., Weekly Agriculture examines the latest news in agriculture and food politics and policy.

item.title: MAHA secured a big pesticide win. What's next?
item.description: The MAHA movement is running a victory lap after a crucial farm bill vote — and warning the Senate not to test it.
item.dc:creator: By Grace Yarrow and Rachel Shin
item.dc:contributor: By Grace Yarrow and Rachel Shin

item.title: Rotisserie chicken: New SNAP staple?
item.description: SNAP benefits have excluded all hot foods for decades, with the exception of certain flexibilities following natural disasters or in states that opt to allow households with elderly or disabled people to use SNAP at restaurants.
item.dc:creator: By Grace Yarrow

item.title: The GOP’s food fight
item.description: The Iran war is forcing Republicans to confront the distressing reality that food prices likely won't drop before the midterm elections.
item.dc:creator: By Grace Yarrow

item.title: E15 dreams
item.description: The clock is ticking for Congress to deliver on permanent year-round E15 promises.
item.dc:creator: By Grace Yarrow and Rachel Shin
item.dc:contributor: By Grace Yarrow and Rachel Shin

item.title: SNAP and the farm bill’s fate
item.description: Senate Ag Democrats want changes to the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program’s cost-sharing model included in the chamber's farm bill.
item.dc:creator: By Rachel Shin

item.title: It’s finally farm bill week
item.description: The House will vote on the farm bill this week, and the Supreme Court will hear arguments on a contentious pesticide labeling case.
item.dc:creator: By Rachel Shin

item.title: The future of WIC’s fruit and veggie benefit
item.description: A popular fruit and vegetable benefit for low-income moms and babies is on the chopping block. Will Congress follow through?
item.dc:creator: By Marcia Brown

item.title: ‘It looked like a ghost town’
item.description: Low staffing at USDA’s Natural Resources Conservation Service is stalling farmers’ regenerative agriculture projects across the country.
item.dc:creator: By Rachel Shin
